Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and seasonal workers including temporary help services workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment.\nDo not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. Fatalities refer to work-related accidents that result in the death of an employee.\n\nLabor Law No. 37 of 2015
Please enter the number of fatalities for the given year.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.
Total (full) disability is the complete loss of ability to work, due to work injury or occupational disease, as determined by medical assessment and defined by Iraqi law.\n\nLabor Law No. 37 of 2015
Please enter the number of Full (Total) Disability cases for the given year.
Partial disability refers to a reduction in the worker’s ability to perform their occupation, as certified by a medical evaluation.\n\nLabor Law No. 37 of 2015
Please enter the number of Partial Disability for the given year.

Occupational diseases are illnesses directly caused by workplace exposure to harmful conditions or agents, officially recognized under Iraqi social security law. \n\nLaw No. 18 of 2023 - Workers’ Retirement and Social Security Law
Please enter the number of Occupational Disease for the given year.
